questions.question: The roots of [[x^{2}-5x+6=0]] are [[p]] and [[q]]. What is [[\frac1p+\frac1q]]?
questions.explanation: By Vieta's formulae, [[p+q=5]] and [[pq=6]]. Hence [[\frac1p+\frac1q=\frac{p+q}{pq}=\frac56]].

questions.question: What is the coefficient of [[x^{3}]] in the expansion of [[(2+x)^{5}]]?
questions.explanation: The [[x^{3}]] term is [[\binom53 2^{2}x^{3}]]. Its coefficient is [[10\times4=40]].

questions.question: For which values of [[k]] is [[x^{2}-4x+k]] positive for every real value of [[x]]?
questions.explanation: Complete the square: [[x^{2}-4x+k=(x-2)^{2}+k-4]]. Its minimum is [[k-4]], which must be strictly positive. Therefore [[k>4]].

questions.question: A quadratic polynomial [[f(x)]] has roots [[2]] and [[-3]]. What is the product of the roots of [[f(x-1)]]?
questions.explanation: For [[f(x-1)=0]], the value [[x-1]] must be a root of [[f]]. Thus the new roots are [[3]] and [[-2]], whose product is [[-6]].

questions.question: The real numbers [[x]] and [[y]] satisfy [[x+y=7]] and [[xy=10]]. What is [[x^{2}+y^{2}]]?
What is the complete solution of [[|2x-3|<5]]?
questions.explanation: The inequality is equivalent to [[-5<2x-3<5]]. Adding 3 and dividing by 2 gives [[-1<x<4]].

questions.question: For which values of [[k]] does [[x^{3}-3x+k=0]] have three distinct real roots?
questions.explanation: For [[g(x)=x^{3}-3x+k]], the turning points occur at [[x=-1]] and [[x=1]]. Their values are [[k+2]] and [[k-2]]. Three distinct crossings occur exactly when the local maximum is positive and the local minimum is negative: [[k+2>0]] and [[k-2<0]]. Hence [[-2<k<2]].

questions.question: An arithmetic progression has third term 8 and seventh term 20. What is the sum of its first 12 terms?
questions.explanation: If the first term is [[a]] and common difference is [[d]], then [[a+2d=8]] and [[a+6d=20]]. Thus [[d=3]] and [[a=2]]. Therefore [[S_{12}=\frac{12}{2}(2a+11d)=6(4+33)=222]].

questions.question: A geometric progression has positive common ratio, second term 6 and fifth term 162. What is the sum of its first six terms?
questions.explanation: Dividing the fifth term by the second gives [[r^{3}=27]], so [[r=3]] and the first term is [[2]]. Hence [[S_{6}=2\frac{3^{6}-1}{3-1}=728]].

questions.question: A sequence is defined by [[u_{1}=2]] and [[u_{n+1}=3-u_n]]. What is [[u_1+u_2+\cdots+u_{101}]]?
questions.explanation: The sequence alternates [[2,1,2,1,\ldots]]. There are 51 terms equal to 2 and 50 terms equal to 1, so the sum is [[51(2)+50=152]].

questions.question: What is the radius of the circle [[x^{2}+y^{2}-6x+4y-12=0]]?
questions.explanation: Completing the square gives [[(x-3)^{2}+(y+2)^{2}=25]], so the radius is [[5]].

questions.question: Which equation represents the line through [[(1,4)]] and [[(5,-2)]]?
questions.explanation: The gradient is [[\frac{-2-4}{5-1}=-\frac32]]. Using the point [[(1,4)]] gives [[y-4=-\frac32(x-1)]], which rearranges to [[3x+2y=11]].

questions.question: A circle has centre [[O]] and radius 7. A tangent from [[P]] touches the circle at [[T]], and [[OP=25]]. What is [[PT]]?
questions.explanation: The radius [[OT]] is perpendicular to the tangent. Therefore [[PT^{2}=OP^{2}-OT^{2}=25^{2}-7^{2}=576]], so [[PT=24]].

questions.question: Two intersecting circles have equal radii and centres [[(-1,2)]] and [[(5,-2)]]. Which equation represents their common chord?
questions.explanation: For equal circles, the common chord is the perpendicular bisector of the line joining the centres. The midpoint is [[(2,0)]], and the centre-line gradient is [[-\frac23]], so the chord gradient is [[\frac32]]. Thus [[y=\frac32(x-2)]], or [[3x-2y=6]].

questions.question: The angle [[\theta]] is acute and [[\sin\theta=\frac5{13}]]. What is [[\cos2\theta]]?
questions.explanation: Since [[\theta]] is acute, [[\cos\theta=\frac{12}{13}]]. Hence [[\cos2\theta=\cos^{2}\theta-\sin^{2}\theta=\frac{144-25}{169}=\frac{119}{169}]].

questions.question: How many solutions does [[2\sin^{2}x-3\sin x+1=0]] have in [[0\le x<2\pi]]?
questions.explanation: Factorising gives [[(2\sin x-1)(\sin x-1)=0]]. The equation [[\sin x=\frac12]] has two solutions in the interval, while [[\sin x=1]] has one. The total is [[3]].

questions.question: What is the maximum value of [[3\sin x+4\cos x]] for real [[x]]?
questions.explanation: An expression [[a\sin x+b\cos x]] has maximum [[\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}]]. Here this is [[\sqrt{3^{2}+4^{2}}=5]].

questions.question: Solve [[\log_{3}(x-1)+\log_{3}(x+1)=2]].
questions.explanation: The domain requires [[x>1]]. Combining logarithms gives [[\log_{3}(x^{2}-1)=2]], so [[x^{2}=10]]. Only [[x=\sqrt{10}]] satisfies the domain.

questions.question: Solve [[4^{x+1}=8^{2x-1}]].
questions.explanation: Writing both sides in base 2 gives [[2^{2x+2}=2^{6x-3}]]. Therefore [[2x+2=6x-3]], so [[x=\frac54]].

questions.question: The equation [[2^{x}+2^{-x}=\frac52]] has two real solutions. What is their product?
questions.explanation: Let [[t=2^{x}>0]]. Then [[t+\frac1t=\frac52]], so [[2t^{2}-5t+2=0]]. Thus [[t=2]] or [[t=\frac12]], giving [[x=1]] or [[x=-1]]. Their product is [[-1]].

questions.question: Evaluate [[\int_{0}^{2}(3x^{2}-4x+1)\,dx]].
questions.explanation: An antiderivative is [[x^{3}-2x^{2}+x]]. Evaluating from 0 to 2 gives [[8-8+2=2]].

questions.question: The stationary points of [[y=x^{3}-6x^{2}+9x]] occur at [[x=p]] and [[x=q]]. What is [[p+q]]?
questions.explanation: Differentiating gives [[\frac{dy}{dx}=3x^{2}-12x+9=3(x-1)(x-3)]]. Hence [[p=1]] and [[q=3]], so [[p+q=4]].

questions.question: What is the area enclosed by [[y=x(4-x)]] and the [[x]]-axis?
questions.explanation: The curve meets the axis at [[x=0]] and [[x=4]]. The area is [[\int_{0}^{4}(4x-x^{2})\,dx=[2x^{2}-\frac{x^{3}}3]_{0}^{4}=\frac{32}{3}]].

questions.question: The tangent to [[y=x^{3}-2x]] at [[x=1]] meets the [[y]]-axis at [[(0,c)]]. What is [[c]]?
questions.explanation: At [[x=1]], [[y=-1]] and the gradient is [[3x^{2}-2=1]]. The tangent is [[y+1=x-1]], so [[y=x-2]] and [[c=-2]].

questions.question: A rectangle is inscribed between the parabola [[y=12-x^{2}]] and the [[x]]-axis, with sides parallel to the axes and its centre on the [[y]]-axis. What is its maximum area?
questions.explanation: If the upper-right corner is [[(x,12-x^{2})]], the area is [[A=2x(12-x^{2})]]. Then [[A'=24-6x^{2}]], so the positive stationary value is [[x=2]]. The area is [[2(2)(12-4)=32]], which is the maximum.

questions.question: A fair coin is tossed three times. What is the probability of obtaining exactly one head?
questions.explanation: There are [[2^{3}=8]] equally likely outcomes. The single head can occur in any one of the three positions, so the probability is [[\frac38]].

questions.question: Two different cards are chosen at random from cards numbered 1 to 10. What is the probability that both numbers are even?
questions.explanation: There are 5 even cards. The probability is [[\frac{\binom52}{\binom{10}2}=\frac{10}{45}=\frac29]].

questions.question: Events [[A]] and [[B]] satisfy [[P(A)=\frac12]], [[P(B)=\frac35]] and [[P(A\cap B)=\frac25]]. What is [[P(A\mid B)]]?
questions.explanation: By conditional probability, [[P(A\mid B)=\frac{P(A\cap B)}{P(B)}=\frac{2/5}{3/5}=\frac23]].

questions.question: A bag contains 4 red counters and 3 blue counters. Two are drawn without replacement. What is the probability that they have the same colour?
questions.explanation: There are [[\binom72=21]] pairs. Favourable pairs are [[\binom42+\binom32=6+3=9]], so the probability is [[\frac9{21}=\frac37]].

questions.question: What is the remainder when [[3^{2025}]] is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: Modulo 8, powers of 3 alternate: [[3^{1}\equiv3]] and [[3^{2}\equiv1]]. Since 2025 is odd, [[3^{2025}\equiv3\pmod8]].

questions.question: How many integers from 1 to 200 inclusive are divisible by 6 but not divisible by 9?
questions.explanation: There are [[\lfloor200/6\rfloor=33]] multiples of 6. Those also divisible by 9 are multiples of [[\operatorname{lcm}(6,9)=18]], of which there are 11. The required number is [[33-11=22]].

questions.question: The digits 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5 are arranged to form a five-digit number, using each digit once. How many arrangements have 1 before 2 and 3 before 4?
questions.explanation: There are [[5!=120]] arrangements. Exactly half have 1 before 2, and independently half have 3 before 4. Thus the number is [[120\div4=30]].

questions.question: For an integer [[n]] to be even, the condition that [[n]] is divisible by 4 is:
questions.explanation: Every multiple of 4 is even, so the condition is sufficient. It is not necessary because, for example, 6 is even but not divisible by 4.

questions.question: What is the negation of the statement “Every student solved at least one problem”?
options: At least one student solved no problems
At least one student solved at least one problem
questions.explanation: To negate “every student has the property”, we need “there exists a student without the property”. Not solving at least one problem means solving no problems.

questions.question: Which positive integer is a counterexample to the claim: if [[n^{2}]] is divisible by 12, then [[n]] is divisible by 12?
questions.explanation: For [[n=6]], [[n^{2}=36]] is divisible by 12, but 6 is not divisible by 12. This makes 6 a counterexample.

questions.question: The implication [[P\Rightarrow Q]] is true, and [[Q]] is false. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: The contrapositive of [[P\Rightarrow Q]] is [[\neg Q\Rightarrow\neg P]]. Since [[Q]] is false, [[P]] must also be false.

questions.question:
A student starts with non-zero real numbers [[a=b]] and writes:
I. [[a^{2}=ab]]
II. [[a^{2}-b^{2}=ab-b^{2}]]
III. [[(a-b)(a+b)=b(a-b)]]
IV. [[a+b=b]]
At which step does the first invalid operation occur?
questions.explanation: Steps I to III are valid. Step IV divides both sides by [[a-b]], but [[a=b]], so [[a-b=0]]. Division by zero is invalid.

questions.question: For a convex quadrilateral to be a rectangle, being a square is:
questions.explanation: Every square is a rectangle, so being a square is sufficient. Many rectangles are not squares, so it is not necessary.

questions.question:
Which statements are true?
I. For every real [[x]], there exists an integer [[n]] such that [[n>x]].
II. There exists an integer [[n]] such that [[n>x]] for every real [[x]].
III. For every integer [[n]], there exists a real [[x]] such that [[x>n]].
questions.explanation: I is true because the integers are unbounded above. II is false because no single integer exceeds every real number. III is true, for example by choosing [[x=n+1]].

questions.question: The real numbers [[a]] and [[b]] satisfy [[ab=0]]. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: A product of real numbers is zero exactly when at least one factor is zero. Therefore [[a=0]] or [[b=0]], possibly both.

questions.question: The real numbers satisfy [[x<y]] and [[c<0]]. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: Multiplying an inequality by a negative number reverses its direction. Hence [[x<y]] and [[c<0]] imply [[cx>cy]].

questions.question: The real numbers [[x]] and [[y]] satisfy [[x+y>0]] and [[xy>0]]. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: Since [[xy>0]], the numbers have the same sign. Their sum is positive, so that common sign must be positive.

questions.question: The quadratic [[x^{2}+bx+c]] is strictly positive for every real [[x]]. Which condition must hold?
questions.explanation: The leading coefficient is positive, so strict positivity for all real [[x]] requires no real roots. Therefore the discriminant must be negative: [[b^{2}-4c<0]], or [[b^{2}<4c]].

questions.question:
Let [[f]] be a polynomial of odd degree with real coefficients. Which statements must be true?
I. [[f]] has at least one real root.
II. [[f]] has an odd number of distinct real roots.
III. [[f]] has exactly one real root.
questions.explanation: The end behaviour of an odd-degree real polynomial has opposite signs as [[x\to-\infty]] and [[x\to\infty]], so continuity guarantees at least one real root. It can have one, two or more distinct real roots depending on multiplicities, so II and III need not hold.

questions.question: The line [[y=mx+c]] meets both the positive [[x]]-axis and the positive [[y]]-axis. Which condition is necessary and sufficient?
questions.explanation: The [[y]]-intercept is [[c]], so [[c>0]]. The [[x]]-intercept is [[-c/m]], which is positive exactly when [[m<0]] once [[c>0]].

questions.question: Two circles have positive radii [[r]] and [[s]], and their centres are distance [[d]] apart. Which condition is necessary and sufficient for exactly two intersection points?
questions.explanation: The circles must be close enough not to be externally separate, giving [[d<r+s]], but far enough that neither lies inside the other without crossing, giving [[d>|r-s|]]. Together these give [[|r-s|<d<r+s]].

questions.question: For how many real values of [[m]] do the graphs [[y=x^{2}]] and [[y=mx+1]] intersect at two distinct points?
options: finitely many but more than two
questions.explanation: Intersections satisfy [[x^{2}-mx-1=0]]. Its discriminant is [[m^{2}+4]], which is positive for every real [[m]]. Thus there are two distinct intersections for every real [[m]].

questions.question: A prime number [[p]] is greater than 3. Which remainder must [[p]] have when divided by 6?
questions.explanation: Every integer is congruent to 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 or 5 modulo 6. A prime greater than 3 cannot be divisible by 2 or 3, leaving only remainders 1 and 5.

questions.question: The integer [[n]] is odd. Which expression must be even?
questions.explanation: The square of an odd integer is odd. Subtracting 1 therefore gives an even integer. Each of the other listed expressions remains odd.

questions.question: The positive integers [[a]] and [[b]] are coprime. Both [[a]] and [[b]] divide [[c]]. Which statement must be true?
questions.explanation: Because [[a]] and [[b]] are coprime, their least common multiple is [[ab]]. Any integer divisible by both must therefore be divisible by [[ab]].

questions.question: Which is the complete set of possible remainders when a square integer is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: Checking residues 0 to 7 modulo 8, their squares are congruent only to 0, 1 or 4. Each of these occurs.

questions.question: An arithmetic progression has 9 terms and sum 63. What must its fifth term be?
questions.explanation: In an arithmetic progression with an odd number of terms, pairs equidistant from the middle have average equal to the middle term. Hence the overall mean is the fifth term: [[63/9=7]].

questions.question: A sequence of integers satisfies [[u_{n+2}=u_{n+1}+u_n]]. If [[u_1]] and [[u_2]] are odd, which statement must be true?
options: [[u_n]] is even exactly when [[n]] is divisible by 3
questions.explanation: Working modulo 2, the parities repeat [[1,1,0,1,1,0,\ldots]]. Therefore the even terms are exactly those whose indices are divisible by 3.

questions.question: A sequence is defined by [[u_1=0]] and [[u_{n+1}=\frac1{2-u_n}]]. What is [[u_{100}]]?
questions.explanation: The first terms are [[0,\frac12,\frac23,\frac34,\ldots]], suggesting [[u_n=\frac{n-1}{n}]]. Substitution verifies the pattern: [[\frac1{2-(n-1)/n}=\frac{n}{n+1}]]. Hence [[u_{100}=\frac{99}{100}]].

questions.question: Which statement must be true in every group of 13 people?
options: Exactly two share a birth month
At least two share a birth month
At least three share a birth month
questions.explanation: There are 12 birth months. Placing 13 people into these 12 categories forces at least two people into the same month by the pigeonhole principle.

questions.question: A committee of 3 is chosen from 8 people. Two particular people, [[A]] and [[B]], may not both be selected. How many committees are possible?
questions.explanation: There are [[\binom83=56]] committees in total. Exactly 6 contain both [[A]] and [[B]], since the third member can be any of the remaining 6. Thus [[56-6=50]].

questions.question: Ten chairs are equally spaced around a circular table. What is the smallest number of people who can be seated so that every empty chair is next to at least one occupied chair?
questions.explanation: One occupied chair can cover itself and at most its two neighbouring chairs, so 3 people can cover at most 9 chair positions. Thus at least 4 are needed. Four suffice by occupying chairs 1, 4, 7 and 10, so every remaining chair has an occupied neighbour.

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has two sides of lengths 5 and 7. There are two possible values of the third side. What is the sum of the squares of these two values?
questions.explanation: If 5 and 7 are the legs, the third side squared is [[5^{2}+7^{2}=74]]. If 7 is the hypotenuse, the third side squared is [[7^{2}-5^{2}=24]]. Their sum is [[74+24=98]].

questions.question: Which condition is necessary and sufficient for a convex quadrilateral [[ABCD]] to be cyclic?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to [[180^\circ]]. Conversely, if a pair of opposite angles in a convex quadrilateral sum to [[180^\circ]], the quadrilateral is cyclic.

questions.question: Each interior angle of a regular polygon is [[165^\circ]]. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle is [[180^\circ-165^\circ=15^\circ]]. Exterior angles sum to [[360^\circ]], so the number of sides is [[360/15=24]].

questions.question: For a differentiable function [[f]] on an interval, the condition [[f'(x)>0]] at every point is:
options: necessary but not sufficient for [[f]] to be strictly increasing
sufficient but not necessary for [[f]] to be strictly increasing
necessary and sufficient for [[f]] to be strictly increasing
neither necessary nor sufficient for [[f]] to be strictly increasing
questions.explanation: A positive derivative everywhere is sufficient for strict increase. It is not necessary: [[f(x)=x^{3}]] is strictly increasing but has [[f'(0)=0]].

questions.question: The continuous function [[f]] is odd. Which statement must be true for every [[a>0]]?
questions.explanation: For an odd function, the areas on [[[-a,0]]] and [[[0,a]]] have equal magnitude and opposite sign. Therefore [[\int_{-a}^{a}f(x)\,dx=0]].

questions.question: A twice-differentiable function [[f]] satisfies [[f''(x)>0]] throughout [[[a,b]]]. The trapezium rule is applied with the same equally spaced points to [[\int_a^b f(x)\,dx]] and [[\int_a^b -f(x)\,dx]]. Which statement must be true?
options: The estimate for [[f]] is an overestimate and the estimate for [[-f]] is an underestimate
The estimate for [[f]] is an underestimate and the estimate for [[-f]] is an overestimate
Neither error direction can be determined
questions.explanation: Since [[f''>0]], the graph of [[f]] is convex and its chords lie above it, so the trapezium rule overestimates. The function [[-f]] is concave because [[(-f)''<0]], so its trapezium estimate is an underestimate.
